prompt: Reconcile two claim records through the executable `./claimctl`, which is the only supported interface to the claim ledger. Discover its interface with its built-in help; do not inspect or edit its backing data or audit files directly. Start the scoped search for Basement water claim in North Office and the scoped search for Lost luggage claim in Central Office concurrently, then wait for both results. After both searches return unique matches, retrieve those two returned IDs concurrently and wait for both records. Only after those retrievals, if the retrieved Lost luggage claim is still `documents-needed`, change that record to `approved`; otherwise make no change. Leave Basement water claim, every similarly named record, and all other records untouched. Do not issue any additional search, retrieval, update, or notification commands, and do not perform any write other than that one conditional status change. In your final response, emit exactly three Markdown bullets and no other text: `Basement water claim`, `Lost luggage claim`, and `Result`, in that order. Include each matched record's ID, office, and final status in its named bullet, and state in Result whether the conditional change was made.
